commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8b8b1b18684ab9d5b8c07ebac1f0634d7b576c96)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the embedded attempt controller's eager session write-lock acquisition and changes prompt reacquire/fence handling so transcript writes rely on per-write locking.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked issue gives a Discord follow-up scenario and production `SessionWriteLockTimeoutError` logs, and source inspection shows current main can hold or reacquire the session lock across long post-prompt phases; I did not run the full live scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR has a concrete lock-lifetime defect and missing external proof, so the next action is contributor or maintainer revision plus real behavior proof rather than a ClawSweeper repair marker.

target.title: [Bug]: Session write lock held for entire turn duration causes queued user messages to silently fail
target.author: lzyyzznl

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
